thesis: Nuclear power is experiencing a global renaissance as countries seek baseload zero-carbon energy. Uranium demand (~204M lbs) already exceeds mine supply (~173M lbs), creating a structural deficit. Prices must reach $100+/lb to incentivize new production.

drivers: New reactor announcements accelerate (China, U.S., EU)
Sprott Physical Trust continues removing supply from market

risks: Nuclear accident freezes global reactor development

base_case.assumptions: Steady reactor demand growth; supply slowly responds; uranium trades $90–110/lb; URA returns 20–30% annually.

catalysts.event: U.S. DOE uranium supply chain funding announcement
Japan Nuclear Regulation Authority restart decision
